summary:
There is no clinical support from available literature indicating that dogs can prime autistic children for therapy. Evidence highlights established human-based interventions focusing on relationship-building and structured environments rather than animal-assisted components. 

Note: This summary strictly adheres to factual content derived directly from the provided information without adding external assumptions.
